Title: v0.1.0 — First Linux release
Summary: The initial packaged release. Twelve authoring surfaces, two-way .tres sync, Diagnostics, Assets gallery + image editor, theming, Electron desktop wrap.
Body:
## What shipped

v0.1.0 is the first packaged Linux AppImage release of Bleepforge. Everything that landed up to this point is bundled here.

### Twelve authoring surfaces

- **Game concept** — Bleepforge-only project pitch / acts / faction roles, the app homepage.
- **Dialogues** — Multi-folder graph view + list view. Drag nodes, draw edges, double-click edges to rename, per-edge dashed/curved/waypoint style. Per-folder layout persistence. Per-choice outgoing handles with type-colored squares. Source-type filter (NPC / Terminal). Right-click create-sequence on empty canvas.
- **Quests** — Objectives + rewards as discriminated unions, auto-managed flag strips, link-out to giver NPC.
- **Items** — `ItemData` + `QuestItemData` polymorphism. Card/list views grouped by category. Live atlas-aware icons read straight from `.tres`.
- **Karma impacts** — Per-faction karma deltas, range-clamped, free-form-id-keyed.
- **NPCs** — Full authoring: portrait, dialogs, quest entries (with offer/accepted/in-progress/turn-in/post-quest dialog refs), loot tables (with pickup picker), casual remarks (balloon refs).
- **Factions** — The four enum-keyed factions with banners + descriptions.
- **Balloons** — The "Hi there!" lines NPCs say when you walk up. Cards mimic in-game speech bubbles; type-speed animates on hover.
- **Game Codex** — Bleepforge-only multi-category notebook with user-defined property schemas (Hazards, Locations, etc.). Never round-tripped to Godot.
- **Assets** — Browse every image in the project. "Used by N" reverse-lookup against `.tres` + `.tscn`. In-app editor with crop, tint, flip, auto-trim, ML / heuristic background removal, "Magic crop" subject detection.
- **Help** — Bleepforge-only documentation library with sidebar and in-page search. 10 seed categories, 56 entries shipped.

### Two-way `.tres` sync

- **Boot-time reconcile** — Every server start rebuilds the JSON cache from `.tres`. If you edit Godot while Bleepforge is off, the next launch picks it up. No drift.
- **Live watcher** — chokidar (with our own 150ms debounce in place of `awaitWriteFinish`) catches every external `.tres` change and reimports the matching JSON in real time. Self-write suppression prevents loops.
- **Save-back writeback** — Save in Bleepforge → JSON written first → matching `.tres` updated atomically (temp file + rename). Round-trip preserves all properties, including ext_resources for textures + scripts + ItemData refs.
- **`_subId` stable identity** for sub-resource arrays (DialogLine, DialogChoice, QuestObjective, QuestReward, KarmaDelta) — reorder-safe writes that don't churn unrelated rows.
- **Orphan ext_resource cleanup** — final post-pass on every save drops any `[ext_resource]` whose id no longer appears in property values.

### Diagnostics page

Six tabs under `/diagnostics` with a severity-aware unified header icon:

- **Integrity** — authored-content checks (dangling FKs, duplicate ids, etc.).
- **Reconcile** — boot-time `.tres` → JSON cache rebuild status, per-domain counts, errored/skipped file paths.
- **Logs** — server-side log buffer (1000-entry ring) with All / Good / Bad filter.
- **Saves** — live SSE-pushed activity feed of every save in both directions.
- **Process** — server identity, uptime, config, resolved Godot project root.
- **Watcher** — chokidar status + recent events feed with per-outcome tagging.

### App-wide search

`Ctrl+K` from anywhere. Substring matching (intentionally not fuzzy — predictable for a single-author corpus) across NPCs, items, quests, karma, factions, dialog sequences, balloons, and Codex entries. Per-kind colored badges, fixed widths, keyboard navigation.

### Theming + global themes

- Eight color themes: dark / light / red / amber / green / cyan / blue / magenta. Plus per-source-type palettes for dialog graphs (NPC / Terminal).
- Eight pixel-art body fonts: Pixelify Sans, Silkscreen, Jersey 10, Tiny5, DotGothic16, Handjet, Workbench, Sixtyfour.
- UI scale + letter spacing knobs.
- Bundled into named "global themes" you can save and switch between. Cross-window theme sync via BroadcastChannel.
- Themed scrollbars + pixel sliders with chunked theme-aware fill.

### Electron desktop wrap

- **Dev mode** — `pnpm dev:desktop` opens an Electron window pointing at the Vite dev server. HMR + SSE preserved.
- **Linux AppImage** — `pnpm dist` produces a single-file ~115MB AppImage. Server bundled via esbuild and booted in-process inside Electron main. User state persists at `~/.config/Bleepforge/`. The Help library seeds from inside the asar on first launch.
- **Chromeless popouts** — Diagnostics / Help / Preferences open in their own dedicated windows with the app header / footer stripped.
- **Cross-window state sync** — preferences (theme, font, etc.) propagate across windows via BroadcastChannel.

### UI polish + safety

- **Splash screen** — real-progress boot bar wired to 3 checkpoints (server / preferences / catalog). Pixel-themed CONTINUE button. Stays on the splash-sized window until dismissed.
- **Toast notifications** — bottom-right stack, hover-pauses, dedupes by id. Sync events from Godot trigger clickable toasts that navigate to the entity.
- **Error boundary** — class component wrapping the entire `Routes` block. Catches render-phase errors and shows a friendly broken-robot screen instead of a white page.
- **404 page** — confused-robot illustration with three navigation links so the user always lands somewhere useful.
- **Empty states** — illustrated cards for every list page (Workshop / Noticeboard / Terminal silent / Bunker) when the corpus is empty.
- **Modal singleton** — confirm + prompt helpers via `showConfirm()` / `showPrompt()`. Replaced every `window.confirm` / `window.prompt`.
- **Context menu** — pixel-themed replacement for the browser's default. Cut / Copy / Paste in inputs, plus per-component custom items.
- **AssetPicker** — server-mediated filesystem browse modal used everywhere an image path is authored. Inline thumbnails. Click rule: click previews, right-click edits.

### Auto-discovery (Phase 1)

Boot reconcile walks the Godot project once and buckets every `.tres` by content (`script_class` for the five script-discriminated domains, `Slug = "..."` presence for items). Adding a new NPC's dialog folder, a new collectible category, etc. — works at next boot with zero code changes. (v0.2.0 extended this idea to every "find this entity's file" path via the ProjectIndex — see [v0.2.0](/help/release-notes/v0-2-0).)

## Notable bumps along the way

The first release wasn't smooth — five distinct Linux landmines hit during AppImage packaging:

1. **Workspace dep resolution** — pnpm-isolated `@bleepforge/shared` exports `.ts` source via `main`, which Node can't load. Fixed by switching the server build to esbuild (inlines workspace deps, externalizes npm deps).
2. **Sandbox flag conflict** — Chromium 130 SIGTRAPs the renderer when `--enable-sandbox` (auto-added by BrowserWindow) and `--no-sandbox` (our global switch) both appear. Fixed by setting `webPreferences.sandbox: false` to match the global switch.
3. **`/dev/shm` refusal** — Fedora 44 sometimes refused shared memory init. Defensive `disable-dev-shm-usage` flag added.
4. **`fs.cpSync` inside asar** — fails with ENOTDIR on `opendirSync`. Rewrote help-library seeding as `readdirSync` + `copyFileSync` recursion.
5. **Express `send` library inside asar** — `res.sendFile` returns 500 NotFoundError for asar paths. Fixed by reading `index.html` once at boot into memory and serving directly via `res.send()`.

A sixth landmine surfaced just after the AppImage was running: a **SIGTRAP on window close** under Linux/Chromium 130 from forced channel cleanup. Fixed by registering a `pagehide` listener that explicitly closes the 4 EventSources + relay BroadcastChannels.

A seventh (electron-builder's `extraMetadata.name` rewrites the source `package.json` in place) was sidestepped by keeping the build config in a separate `electron-builder.json` file.

All seven are documented in CLAUDE.md under "Electron desktop wrap" so the next platform target lands cleanly.

> tip: see [v0.2.0](/help/release-notes/v0-2-0) for what's new since this release.
